explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Reliance Industries' reports reference the company's investments and initiatives in AI, indicating strategic decision-making and management regarding AI development and deployment. The company has participated in a project to develop frameworks for governing healthcare chatbots, involving diverse stakeholders to establish responsible AI practices and create structured approaches for AI deployment. Furthermore, reports highlight the testing of an AI-based algorithm for large-scale image analysis and Reliance Jio's use of smart sensors for energy optimization, suggesting a framework for managing and overseeing AI deployment.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The "Business Responsibility Report 2021-22" by Reliance Industries provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The report mentions the company's investment and initiatives in AI, specifically highlighting the testing of an AI-based algorithm for large-scale image analysis in areas like digital agriculture and product quality assessment. This indicates strategic decision-making and management regarding AI development and deployment.
relevant_sources.title: Business Responsibility Report 2021-22

relevant_sources.summary: This World Economic Forum report, "The AI Governance Journey: Development and Opportunities," provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The report details Reliance Industries' participation in a project to develop frameworks for governing healthcare chatbots, which involved bringing together diverse stakeholders to establish responsible AI practices. This initiative highlights a focus on creating structured approaches and best practices for AI deployment.
relevant_sources.title: The AI Governance Journey: Development and Opportunities

relevant_sources.summary: The Reliance Industries Integrated Annual Report 2023-24 provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. This report details investments and initiatives in AI, indicating a strategic direction and decision-making process that aligns with governance principles. The evidence is categorized as POLICY, highlighting the company's commitment to AI development and deployment.
relevant_sources.title: Reliance Industries Integrated Annual Report 2023-24

relevant_sources.summary: The Reliance BRSR Report 2024-25 provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. This report details investments and initiatives in AI, demonstrating strategic decision-making and management commitment to AI innovation, which are key aspects of responsible AI governance.
relevant_sources.title: Reliance BRSR Report 2024-25

relevant_sources.summary: The "Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report 2022-23" provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The report's mention of investments and initiatives in AI, particularly Reliance Jio's use of smart sensors and connected intelligence for energy optimization, indicates a strategic approach and commitment to leveraging AI technologies. This suggests a framework for managing and overseeing AI deployment within the organization.
relevant_sources.title: Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report 2022-23

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: Reliance's published materials indicate strategic decision-making and management regarding AI development and deployment, addressing governance at the policy level. This pillar is the only one with documented evidence among the 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars, based on a review of 15 publicly available sources. No qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
